title: SERVICES - 211info
text: CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides a variety of domestic violence, sexual assault, and human trafficking survivor support services such as support groups, 911 cell phones, court accompaniment, community education, the Address Confidentiality Program, and information and referral to other social services. 605 Center Street NE Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Survivors of domestic violence, sexual abuse, human trafficking, and/or stalking, regardless of gender identity or sexual orientation Hours: Walk in: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-5pm Phone: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (503) 399-7722 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (503) 399-7722 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for more information FEES: None DOCUMENTS: None LANGUAGES: English, Spanish, interpretation by arrangement AREA SERVED: Marion County TRAVEL/LOCATION INFORMATION: Located one block from the downtown bus mall. Parking is available in the lot at the front of the building along Center Street. Services: Confidential Address Protection Programs Crime Victim Accompaniment Services Domestic Violence Support Groups Human Trafficking Prevention Human Trafficking Support Groups Mobile Devices Sexual Assault Prevention Sexual Assault/Incest Support Groups Specialized Information and Referral * Domestic Violence Issues Specialized Information and Referral * Human Trafficking Issues Specialized Information and Referral * Sexual Assault Issues Spouse/Intimate Partner Abuse Prevention Other Services Offered At This Location: HOUSING/SHELTER - EMERGENCY SHELTER MENTAL HEALTH ASSESSMENT AND TREATMENT - 24-HOUR CRISIS LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. CENTER FOR HOPE AND SAFETY
